mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a robust labor market and expanding economic activity, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a specific threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy aimed at cooling an overheating economy. This typically leads to higher borrowing costs, reduced investment, and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, the S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with a low unemployment rate signal robust consumer confidence and purchasing power. This joint strength drives corporate revenues and profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If periods of high RSAFS growth and low UNRATE do not correlate with significantly positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns (e.g., Rank IC < 0.05) over any 4-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, a sustained increase in industrial production indicates a rebound in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity. This economic revitalization is bullish for corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, during identified recovery periods, INDPRO increases are not followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid decline in housing starts signals a weakening in the residential construction sector and broader economic demand. This contraction in a key cyclical industry portends negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If a >10% month-over-month drop in HOUST is followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over any 5-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent and accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real wages and corporate profit margins, leading to reduced consumer spending and investor confidence. This inflationary pressure drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If, after 3 consecutive months of CPIAUCSL rising > 0.4%, the S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate indicates a significant deterioration in the labor market, signaling an impending or ongoing economic contraction. This typically leads to substantial declines in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If a rise in UNRATE of >0.5pp over 3 months is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 35% of triggered instances over any 7-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate profitability and supports positive S&P 500 returns over longer horizons.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observed instances over a 10-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.
